raw_user_messages:
<codex_delegation>
  <source_thread_id>019eaf56-91db-7c40-b740-7435313502d3</source_thread_id>
  <input>For each independent project under D:\project\.codex-eval-full\REGRESSION-20260612-01\workspaces\E12\state_matrix\scenarios, recover the compact state, build the deterministic ADR metadata projection, validate replacement relations and PLAN references, and report blocking conflicts. Read only the bodies needed for the current task.</input>
</codex_delegation>
commentary: I’m using the `codex-project-workflow` skill because this is a multi-project recovery and validation pass. I’ll first read its operating rules, then inspect only the scenario-level files needed to identify each project’s state and expected outputs.
This task triggers the workflow’s recovery and path-verification protocol. I’m loading that narrow reference now, then I’ll map the scenario directories without opening project bodies prematurely.

There are 12 independent fixtures, each with a compact `PLAN.md` and ADR ledger; only scenario 12 also has PRD/audit material. Per the recovery contract, I’ll read every compact PLAN first and use those references to decide whether any non-metadata body sections are actually needed.

The first metadata probe found no conventional YAML-style keys, so the ledger uses a different compact format. I’m opening one representative ledger to identify its structural markers, then I’ll extract only those markers across all scenarios.

The ledger is valid UTF-8; only the PowerShell display path garbled the Chinese labels. I’ve confirmed the structural fields are `治理状态` and `替代关系`, so the projection can be built deterministically from ADR headings plus those metadata lines, while excluding decision and reason text entirely.

The metadata inventory already distinguishes the intended matrix: valid graph, unilateral links, directional mismatch, duplicates, missing state, invalid targets, self-reference, cycles, status mismatch, PLAN cross-check, and selective loading. Before final validation, I’m reading only the one decision body and PRD section explicitly required by scenario 12’s `Next` instruction; the audit archive remains untouched.

One validator detail needed tightening: repeated identical `replaced-by` declarations are a duplicate-field conflict, but they do not make the target semantically ambiguous. I’m normalizing identical values in the projection while preserving the duplicate declaration as the sole blocker, avoiding a misleading secondary status error.
final_response:
Recovered all 12 compact states and projected 28 ADR declarations using exactly:

`id, title, governance_state, replaces_ids, replaced_by_id, body_anchor`

Anchors use `docs/DECISIONS.md#<normalized-id-and-title>`.

**Results**
- `01-valid-graph`: Valid. Active: `ADR-004`, `ADR-005`, `ADR-006`; PLAN references valid.
- `02-unilateral`: Blocked. `ADR-001` points to `ADR-002`, but reverse `replaces` relation is absent.
- `03-direction-mismatch`: Blocked. `ADR-001` names `ADR-003`; `ADR-003` only replaces `ADR-002`.
- `04-duplicate-relation`: Blocked. Identical `replaced-by ADR-002` fields at `DECISIONS.md:7-8`.
- `05-duplicate-id`: Blocked. `ADR-001` declared twice at `DECISIONS.md:3` and `:11`.
- `06-missing-state`: Blocked. `ADR-001` lacks `governance_state`.
- `07-invalid-target`: Blocked. `ADR-001` replaces nonexistent `ADR-999`.
- `08-self-reference`: Blocked. `ADR-001` both replaces and is replaced by itself.
- `09-cycle`: Blocked. Cycle: `ADR-001 -> ADR-002 -> ADR-001`.
- `10-status-mismatch`: Blocked. `ADR-001` is confirmed but has `replaced-by ADR-002`.
- `11-plan-crosscheck`: Blocked. PLAN line 14 lists replaced `ADR-003` as active and references missing `ADR-999`.
- `12-selective-loading`: Valid. `ADR-001` and `ADR-002` are active; all PLAN references resolve.

For scenario 12, only the referenced `ADR-002` body and PRD `Retention Rules` section were loaded. Both specify 30-day retention. `audits/ARCHIVE.md` was not read.

No files were modified.

---
name: codex-project-workflow
description: Structure and govern Codex work for new or ambiguous projects, multi-step implementation, professional research, solution comparison, architecture and planning, third-party downloads, high-risk changes, project recovery, or requests for a rigorous workflow. Also use for ordinary multi-step project tasks when research, confirmation, implementation-path verification, recovery, or coordination may matter. Do not use for simple questions or explicit low-risk one-step edits or commands.
---

# Codex Project Workflow

## Route

1. Inspect first. Keep goal, constraints, and completion test. Ask only blocking decisions.
2. From the repo root containing this skill, run `python .agents/skills/codex-project-workflow/scripts/read_reference.py NAME "Execution Rules" "Output Requirements"`. Use `research` for unstable evidence/options; `governance` for third-party assets, installs, execution, private networking, approvals, agents, or durable changes; `verification` for writes, recovery, production/core paths, or path proof. Load each triggered protocol only.
3. Never list headings, scan skill directories, or read references directly. Report helper failure; it waives no gate.
4. Agents suit three streams, conflicting evidence, or high failure cost. Load governance, propose count/roles, ownership, parallel/main work, benefit, cost, risks, and fallback, then ask. Only explicit acceptance starts them; safe unrelated work may continue.

## Safeguards

- Never invent execution, sources, tests, or verification.
- Before writes map entry, state, boundaries, and bypasses. Freeze an evidence-bound contract first for production routing, persistence/schema/migration, architecture, core capability, alternate final output, explicit path proof, or high impact. Never borrow a neighboring or shadow implementation.
- Safety, authorization, and verification override budgets.

## Finish

V

Exit code: 124
Wall time: 10.4 seconds
Output:
command timed out after 10357 milliseconds
## Execution Rules

- Create a formal implementation contract before the first relevant write when any trigger applies: production entry or routing changes; state authority, persistence, schema, or migration changes; architecture boundary or ownership changes; new, copied, or replacement core capability; a direct alternate final-output path; explicit user path proof; or high-impact commitment.
- Freeze the contract from independent evidence before writing. If the source, version, entry, state update, save path, or ownership cannot be proven, stop as implementation-path unverified; do not borrow a sibling, neighboring fixture, helper, or post-hoc contract.
- File count, module count, tests plus code, and mechanical cross-file edits do not trigger a formal contract by themselves. When every trigger is false, keep a one-to-three-line internal path constraint.
- Bind contract claims to independent project evidence such as confirmed docs, registration points, callers, runtime entry points, or state definitions. Preserve later contract differences.
- Test through the formal product entry point when applicable. Mock, spy, coverage, or direct module calls may supplement but cannot alone prove the product path.
- Verify result and implementation path separately. An output can be correct while the implementation is unacceptable.
- For recovery, read compact `PLAN.md` first. The initial ADR projection is exactly `id`, `title`, `governance_state`, `replaces_ids`, `replaced_by_id`, and `body_anchor`; metadata extraction must exclude reason and decision-body text. Load only referenced ADR bodies and PRD sections afterward.

## Output Requirements

Report user action, product entry point, core path, state or persistence change, result, tests, auxiliary scripts, deviations, and unverified paths. If path evidence is insufficient, say: result verified, implementation path unverified.

plan.step: Inventory independent scenario projects and compact state files
Recover PLAN state and build ADR metadata projections
Validate replacement relations and PLAN references
Report blocking conflicts and verified outputs

arguments.code: var root='D:/project/.codex-eval-full/REGRESSION-20260612-01/workspaces/E12/state_matrix/scenarios'; var dirs=(await fs.readdir(root,{withFileTypes:true})).filter(x=>x.isDirectory()).map(x=>x.name).sort(); var rawMeta={}; for (var d of dirs){var s=await fs.readFile(`${root}/${d}/docs/DECISIONS.md`,'utf8'); var lines=s.split(/\r?\n/); var recs=[]; var cur=null; for (var line of lines){var h=line.match(/^##\s+(ADR-\d+):\s*(.+?)\s*$/); if(h){cur={id:h[1],title:h[2],governance:[],relations:[],heading:line};recs.push(cur);continue;} if(!cur)continue; var g=line.match(/^-\s*治理状态：\s*(.*?)\s*$/); if(g)cur.governance.push(g[1]); var r=line.match(/^-\s*替代关系：\s*(.*?)\s*$/); if(r)cur.relations.push(r[1]); } rawMeta[d]=recs;} nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(rawMeta,null,2));

arguments.code: function slugHeading(id,title){return `docs/DECISIONS.md#${(`${id}-${title}`).toLowerCase().normalize('NFKD').replace(/[\u0300-\u036f]/g,'').replace(/[^\p{L}\p{N}\s-]/gu,'').trim().replace(/\s+/g,'-').replace(/-+/g,'-')}`;} function parseRelations(items){var replaces=[];var replacedBy=[];var explicitNone=0;var invalid=[]; for(var it of items){var v=it.value.trim();if(v==='无'){explicitNone++;continue;}var a=v.match(/^replaces\s+(.+)$/i);if(a){for(var id of (a[1].match(/ADR-\d+/g)||[]))replaces.push({id,line:it.line});continue;}var b=v.match(/^replaced-by\s+(ADR-\d+)$/i);if(b){replacedBy.push({id:b[1],line:it.line});continue;}invalid.push(it);}return{replaces,replacedBy,explicitNone,invalid};} var results={}; for(var d of dirs){var decPath=`${root}/${d}/docs/DECISIONS.md`;var planPath=`${root}/${d}/docs/PLAN.md`;var s=await fs.readFile(decPath,'utf8');var lines=s.split(/\r?\n/);var recs=[];var cur=null;for(var i=0;i<lines.length;i++){var line=lines[i];var h=line.match(/^##\s+(ADR-\d+):\s*(.+?)\s*$/);if(h){cur={id:h[1],title:h[2],headingLine:i+1,governance:[],relations:[]};recs.push(cur);continue;}if(!cur)continue;var g=line.match(/^-\s*治理状态：\s*(.*?)\s*$/);if(g)cur.governance.push({value:g[1],line:i+1});var r=line.match(/^-\s*替代关系：\s*(.*?)\s*$/);if(r)cur.relations.push({value:r[1],line:i+1});} var idGroups=new Map();for(var r of recs){if(!idGroups.has(r.id))idGroups.set(r.id,[]);idGroups.get(r.id).push(r);}var diagnostics=[];for(var [id,grp] of idGroups)if(grp.length>1)diagnostics.push({code:'DUPLICATE_ID',at:grp.map(x=>x.headingLine),detail:`${id} declared ${grp.length} times`}); var unique=new Map([...idGroups].filter(([,g])=>g.length===1).map(([id,g])=>[id,g[0]]));for(var r of recs){r.rel=parseRelations(r.relations);if(r.governance.length!==1)diagnostics.push({code:'GOVERNANCE_STATE_CARDINALITY',at:[r.headingLine,...r.governance.map(x=>x.line)],detail:`${r.id} has ${r.governance.length} governance states`});var relKeys=r.relations.map(x=>x.value.toLowerCase());for(var k of new Set(relKeys)){var hits=r.relations.filter(x=>x.value.toLowerCase()===k);if(hits.length>1)diagnostics.push({code:'DUPLICATE_RELATION',at:hits.map(x=>x.line),detail:`${r.id}: ${hits[0].value}`});}if(r.rel.invalid.length)diagnostics.push({code:'INVALID_RELATION_SYNTAX',at:r.rel.invalid.map(x=>x.line),detail:r.id});if(r.rel.explicitNone&&r.relations.length>1)diagnostics.push({code:'NONE_WITH_RELATION',at:r.relations.map(x=>x.line),detail:r.id});if(r.rel.replacedBy.length>1)diagnostics.push({code:'MULTIPLE_REPLACED_BY',at:r.rel.replacedBy.map(x=>x.line),detail:r.id});for(var e of [...r.rel.replaces.map(x=>({...x,kind:'replaces'})),...r.rel.replacedBy.map(x=>({...x,kind:'replaced-by'}))]){if(e.id===r.id)diagnostics.push({code:'SELF_REFERENCE',at:[e.line],detail:`${r.id} ${e.kind} itself`});if(!unique.has(e.id))diagnostics.push({code:'INVALID_TARGET',at:[e.line],detail:`${r.id} ${e.kind} ${e.id}, target missing or ambiguous`});}} for(var r of recs){if(idGroups.get(r.id)?.length!==1)continue;for(var e of r.rel.replaces){var target=unique.get(e.id);if(target&&e.id!==r.id&&!target.rel.replacedBy.some(x=>x.id===r.id))diagnostics.push({code:'UNILATERAL_REPLACES',at:[e.line,target.headingLine],detail:`${r.id} replaces ${e.id}, but ${e.id} does not point back`});}for(var e of r.rel.replacedBy){var source=unique.get(e.id);if(source&&e.id!==r.id&&!source.rel.replaces.some(x=>x.id===r.id))diagnostics.push({code:'UNILATERAL_REPLACED_BY',at:[e.line,source.headingLine],detail:`${r.id} replaced-by ${e.id}, but ${e.id} does not replace it`});}var state=r.governance[0]?.value;var rb=r.rel.replacedBy.length===1?r.rel.replacedBy[0].id:null;if(state==='已确认'&&rb)diagnostics.push({code:'STATUS_MISMATCH',at:[r.governance[0].line,r.rel.replacedBy[0].line],detail:`${r.id} is confirmed but has replaced-by ${rb}`});if(state==='已替代'&&!rb)diagnostics.push({code:'STATUS_MISMATCH',at:[r.governance[0].line],detail:`${r.id} is replaced but lacks replaced-by`});} var adj=new Map([...unique.keys()].map(x=>[x,[]]));for(var [id,r] of unique)for(var e of r.rel.replaces)if(unique.has(e.id)&&e.id!==id)adj.get(id).push(e.id);var color=new Map(),stack=[];var cycleSet=new Set();function dfs(n){color.set(n,1);stack.push(n);for(var m of adj.get(n)){if(!color.has(m))dfs(m);else if(color.get(m)===1){var j=stack.indexOf(m);cycleSet.add([...stack.slice(j),m].join(' -> '));}}stack.pop();color.set(n,2);}for(var n of adj.keys())if(!color.has(n))dfs(n);for(var c of cycleSet)diagnostics.push({code:'REPLACEMENT_CYCLE',at:[],detail:c});var plan=await fs.readFile(planPath,'utf8');var planLines=plan.split(/\r?\n/);var planRefs=[];for(var i=0;i<planLines.length;i++)for(var id of (planLines[i].match(/ADR-\d+/g)||[]))planRefs.push({id,line:i+1,text:planLines[i].trim()});for(var pr of planRefs){var grp=idGroups.get(pr.id);if(!grp)diagnostics.push({code:'PLAN_MISSING_REFERENCE',file:'PLAN.md',at:[pr.line],detail:pr.id});else if(grp.length!==1)diagnostics.push({code:'PLAN_AMBIGUOUS_REFERENCE',file:'PLAN.md',at:[pr.line],detail:pr.id});else if(grp[0].governance[0]?.value!=='已确认'||grp[0].rel.replacedBy.length)diagnostics.push({code:'PLAN_INACTIVE_REFERENCE',file:'PLAN.md',at:[pr.line],detail:`${pr.id} is not active`});}var projection=recs.map(r=>({id:r.id,title:r.title,governance_state:r.governance.length===1?r.governance[0].value:null,replaces_ids:r.rel.replaces.map(x=>x.id),replaced_by_id:r.rel.replacedBy.length===1?r.rel.replacedBy[0].id:null,body_anchor:slugHeading(r.id,r.title)}));results[d]={projection,plan_refs:planRefs.map(x=>x.id),diagnostics};} nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify(results,null,2));
